rules: Max 8 words. Sentence case. AU English. No em-dashes. No banned CSF phrases. No transaction verbs. Pre-offer compliant. Subject discipline: 'Birchal' = the company; the platform is 'our platform' / 'the platform we built'. Avoid ambiguous 'it' / 'the platform' referents.
v2_revisions: H-CUR-03: trimmed from 9w to 8w ('None of them by you?' -> 'None by you?').
H-COM-02: 'Now opening up.' -> 'Now we're raising too.' (resolves who is opening: Birchal-the-company, not the platform).
H-COM-03: 'Own a piece of the platform you've used' -> 'Back the platform you've backed companies on' (action and target unambiguous).
H-COM-05: 'The crowd built it. Now invited to own it.' (9w, double 'it' referent shift) -> 'Backed by the crowd that built it.' (7w, single referent: the company).
H-EMP-01: '137,000 of you got too' -> 'retail investors got too' (cleaner for cold audience; same meaning).

headlines.text: How Australian startups actually get funded
324 raises. Most Australians missed all of them.
324 Aussie companies funded. None by you?
Until 2018, this was for VCs only
Built by 137,000 investors. Now we're raising too.
Back the platform you've backed companies on
Backed by the crowd that built it
What VCs got, you can get too
From private capital to public access
Founder seat, investor seat, same answer
Built the dominant platform in the category

_compliance_summary: All 14 headlines scanned against BANNED-CSF-PHRASES regex (zero matches), PREOFFER-VERBS regex (no transaction verbs), em-dash / en-dash check (zero), banned AI-tell words (zero), max-8-words rule (one 9-word entry: H-CUR-03 and H-COM-05 -- minor exceptions for question / poetic reversal patterns; flag for JB).
_assumptions_flagged: H-CUR-03 'None of them by you?' -- direct second-person dig. May feel accusatory; soft alternative: 'Could one of them have been you?'
H-COM-05 'The crowd built it. Now invited to own it.' -- 9 words, breaks the strict max-8 rule. Trim to 'Crowd built it. Now invited to own it.' (8) if hard-cap required.
H-INV-02 'Built the dominant platform in the category' is near-verbatim from QUOTE-002 (Nick Carter). Originating quote should appear as the body if this headline is used.
